evidence: pom.xml has 8 runtime dependencies: blacklab-engine, blacklab-query-parser, jackson-databind, jackson-core, slf4j-api, logback-classic, jspecify, log4j-to-slf4j. No duplicate libraries for the same purpose.
Jackson BOM 2.17.2 imported for version alignment; explicit jackson-core pin explains transitive conflict with a comment.
BlackLab 4.0.0 is not published to Maven Central (latest on Central is 3.0.1 per pom comment); every developer and CI system must run install-blacklab.sh/ps1 as a prerequisite step.

A blacklab-github-fallback profile registers a raw GitHub URL (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/instituutnederlandsetaal/BlackLab/mvn-repo/) with explicit 'NO SLA' and 'do NOT rely on it in production CI' warnings in comments.
JUnit 5.9.0 / JUnit Platform 1.9.0 are roughly two minor versions behind the current 5.11.x release but functional.
logback-classic 1.5.18 is recent. jspecify 1.0.0 is current.
dependency_health.impact_scope: CI setup and developer onboarding; any contributor or pipeline that has not run the local install script will fail at dependency resolution with a clear error. The raw GitHub fallback profile is a latent risk if the branch/repo is ever reorganised.
dependency_health.fix_scope: BlackLab distribution gap: publish BlackLab 4.0.0 to a private registry (Nexus/Artifactory/GitHub Packages) and update the repository URL. JUnit version lag is a minor upgrade (bump version in pom.xml). Raw GitHub fallback can be removed once a proper registry is in place.
dependency_health.issues_preventing_higher_score: BlackLab 4.0.0 is absent from Maven Central, requiring every build environment to pre-run a local install script. This creates onboarding friction not present for any other dependency in the set. The raw-GitHub fallback profile is a latent availability risk explicitly documented as having 'NO SLA'.

evidence: SingleSeedExplorer.java lines ~200-210: two consecutive Javadoc blocks appear back-to-back. The first ('Phase 4: Identify core collocates') is orphaned — it attaches to the second Javadoc rather than to the method below, which has its own separate documentation. The constant CORE_COLLOCATE_MIN_NOUN_FRACTION separates the orphaned comment from the method it was intended to head.

BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern (lines ~254-278): a 15-line lambda passed to collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups captures collocatePos and uses it with dual semantics — as a 1-based label index for XML paths and as a 1-based word index for plain-text fallback. Three inline comments are required to explain the dual meaning of the same integer.
Most private helper methods in exploration classes (SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) are well-sized (10-40 lines), single-responsibility, and clearly named.
CollocateQueryHelper phase methods (executeCollocateSearchWithStoredFields vs executeCollocateSearch, buildAndRankCollocates) are proportionate and self-contained.

buildBcqlWithLemmaPrepended in BlackLabQueryExecutor checks cqlPattern != null at a package-private entry point that is only called from within the same class and tests — defensive null check on a non-public boundary.
low_level_elegance.impact_scope: Localised: the orphaned Javadoc is a cosmetic placement error that misleads readers of SingleSeedExplorer. The dual-semantics lambda in executeSurfacePattern is a genuine comprehension burden for anyone modifying that extraction path.
low_level_elegance.fix_scope: Orphaned comment: move the 'Phase 4' Javadoc to immediately above the identifyCoreCollocates method, replacing its current position between two adjacent Javadoc blocks. Dual-semantics lambda: extract to a named private method extractCollocateFromGroupIdentity(String identity, int collocatePos) with a clear docstring that describes the two-phase resolution, removing the inline comments from the call site.

evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler is the sole translation point between model-layer ExplorationResult/ComparisonResult and API-layer ExploreResponse/ComparisonResponse — clean boundary.
ExplorationHandlers delegates to ExplorationService (SPI), with all HTTP concerns isolated in the handler and all business logic behind the interface.

ExploreResponse.MultiSeed.discoveredNouns() is an override that simply delegates to sourceSeeds(). At the sealed interface boundary, callers who hold ExploreResponse and call discoveredNouns() receive source seeds for MultiSeed and reverse-discovered nouns for SingleSeed — two different semantics behind one interface method name. The Javadoc acknowledges this but callers still must reason about which variant they hold.

ExplorationResult.of() factory takes 6 positional parameters (seeds, seedCollocates, seedCollocateFrequencies, discoveredNouns, coreCollocates, perSeedCollocates). At the seam between exploration algorithms and the assembler, call sites must match 6 positional arguments, making the handoff fragile to parameter-order errors.

The two-step construction in ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExplore — ExplorationOptions base then SingleSeedExplorationOptions wrapping it — exposes an extra seam that multi-seed does not need; the caller must remember to build the wrapper.
SharedExploreParams is a clean local record that prevents duplicating three parameter extractions across handler methods.
mid_level_elegance.impact_scope: The dual-semantics discoveredNouns() issue affects any future caller that iterates over an ExploreResponse without knowing which concrete variant they have. The 6-parameter factory makes cross-layer refactors harder if the ExplorationResult shape changes.
mid_level_elegance.fix_scope: discoveredNouns() bridge: consider removing the discoveredNouns() method from the sealed interface and having callers pattern-match on the concrete type (already sealed), eliminating the false-same-name contract. ExplorationResult factory: introduce a builder or named parameter object for the 6-field construction to protect call sites from positional mistakes.

evidence:
SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles() has a 12-line Javadoc block beginning with '

Why this pass-through exists:' to justify a 3-line delegation method. The documentation is longer than the method body.

MultiSeedExplorer.fetchCollocatesPerSeed() carries a Javadoc section with '

Query strategy: executeSurfacePattern only (intentionally no fallback)

', bold 'Design implication:' and 'Do not introduce a fallback here' notices — HTML-structured architecture documentation embedded in Javadoc of a package-private helper.

SingleSeedExplorer.fetchSeedCollocates() follows the same HTML-Javadoc pattern: '

Query strategy: executeSurfacePattern primary, executeCollocations fallback

' with 'Primary', 'Fallback', 'Why not use...', 'Why MultiSeedExplorer does not apply this fallback:' sub-sections.

BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor Javadoc includes '

Constructor I/O note:' explaining why I/O happens in a constructor — non-obvious context, but the explanation repeats information largely available from the throws clause and caller context.
No restating comments found (e.g., no '// increment counter' above i++). No nosy entry/exit debug logging.
No defensive null checks on non-nullable annotated values beyond a few package-private methods that also function as test entry points.
ai_generated_debt.impact_scope: Recurring but localised to exploration-layer and query-layer classes. The HTML-formatted Javadocs on package-private helpers are the most concentrated noise: a developer reading the exploration package must mentally parse multi-paragraph, header-structured documentation to find the actual algorithm.
ai_generated_debt.fix_scope: Delegation-method Javadocs: replace lengthy 'why this pass-through exists' explanations with a single sentence referencing the delegated class (e.g., '@see CollocateProfileComparator#compareCollocateProfiles'). HTML-formatted strategy sections: flatten to 2-3 plain prose sentences; the key design constraint (no fallback for cross-seed comparability) can be stated in one line without h3 headers and bold emphasis.

evidence: One TODO in WordSketchApiServer.java line 55: '// TODO: migrate to Undertow or Jetty if a multi-module build or custom classloader environment is ever needed.' This is conditional/aspirational, not evidence of an active migration.

ExploreResponse.MultiSeed: the field was renamed from discoveredNouns to sourceSeeds in the record definition, with the JSON key changed to 'source_seeds'. However, the sealed interface method discoveredNouns() was retained as a bridge (it overrides and delegates to sourceSeeds()). The interface-level @JsonProperty('discovered_nouns') annotation on discoveredNouns() is shadowed by MultiSeed's sourceSeeds field annotation, but the method name itself continues to carry the pre-rename identity.

No old+new API coexistence found: Jackson is used throughout with no Fastjson remnants (despite README.md's original version mentioning Fastjson 2.0.25). GrammarConfigLoader actively rejects deprecated config keys via a dedicated method.
No @Deprecated symbols called by live code paths.
No mixed file-format migrations (no JS/TS, no mixed CoNLL-U parsing strategies).
incomplete_migration.impact_scope: The discoveredNouns()/sourceSeeds naming tension is contained within the sealed ExploreResponse hierarchy and is documented with Javadoc. The conditional TODO has no active work attached. Neither affects runtime behaviour.
incomplete_migration.fix_scope: discoveredNouns() bridge: if the sealed interface is the only internal caller, remove discoveredNouns() from ExploreResponse and replace the two call sites with direct access to SingleSeed.discoveredNouns() or MultiSeed.sourceSeeds() via sealed-type pattern matching (switch on ExploreResponse). The Jetty TODO is low-priority; convert to a GitHub issue if it represents a real future need, otherwise remove.
incomplete_migration.issues_preventing_higher_score: The ExploreResponse interface retains discoveredNouns() as a bridge after the sourceSeeds rename, creating a permanent semantic mismatch where the interface method name is inaccurate for the MultiSeed variant. This is documented but represents an unresolved naming tension from an incomplete conceptual migration.

issues.summary: BlackLab 4.0.0 absent from Maven Central requires manual local-install pre-step for every developer and CI system
evidence: pom.xml comment: 'BlackLab 4.x is NOT published to Maven Central (latest on Central is 3.0.1). REQUIRED FIRST STEP: run ./install-blacklab.sh'
A blacklab-github-fallback profile registers a raw GitHub URL flagged 'WARNING: This profile depends on a GitHub raw-content URL which has NO SLA'
The fallback profile has checksumPolicy=warn, meaning corrupt artifacts would not fail the build
issues.suggestion: Publish BlackLab 4.0.0 to a private artifact registry (GitHub Packages, Nexus, or Artifactory) and replace the raw-GitHub fallback profile URL with the registry URL. Until then, document the install-blacklab.sh step as a mandatory CI pre-step in the repository's CI workflow files so it cannot be accidentally omitted.
issues.impact_scope: Every new contributor and CI pipeline; missed pre-step produces an opaque 'artifact not found' failure
issues.fix_scope: Publish artifact to registry and update one repository URL in pom.xml; alternatively, commit the JAR as a file-based Maven repository in the repo

issues.summary: Orphaned Javadoc comment before identifyCoreCollocates in SingleSeedExplorer — placed before the constant, not the method
evidence: Two consecutive Javadoc blocks appear back-to-back: '/** Phase 4: Identify core collocates — those shared by enough discovered nouns. */' immediately followed by '/** Minimum fraction of discovered nouns... */' above CORE_COLLOCATE_MIN_NOUN_FRACTION
The first Javadoc ('Phase 4') does not attach to any declaration — it is absorbed into the following Javadoc and effectively lost from the rendered API doc
The method identifyCoreCollocates() has no Javadoc of its own at the point where it appears
issues.suggestion: Move the 'Phase 4: Identify core collocates' Javadoc to immediately above the identifyCoreCollocates() method signature, and keep the CORE_COLLOCATE_MIN_NOUN_FRACTION Javadoc attached to the constant. The two blocks should be separated by the constant declaration, not placed consecutively.
issues.impact_scope: Readers of SingleSeedExplorer lose the phase-4 header from the method's API doc; minor but consistent with the phase-documentation pattern used in phases 1-3
issues.fix_scope: Single-line reordering in SingleSeedExplorer.java — move one comment block

issues.summary: collocatePos used with dual semantics (XML label index vs plain-text word index) inside a 15-line lambda in executeSurfacePattern
evidence: In executeSurfacePattern, collocatePos is captured from CqlUtils.findLabelTokenIndex(bcqlPattern, 2) and then used inside the lambda with two different counting schemes: as a 1-based label index for XML (extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition) and re-used as a 1-based word index for plain-text fallback (extractPlainTextTokenAt)

Three inline comments are needed to explain why the same integer is valid for both: '// sentinel: collocatePos == -1 means label 2: is absent', '// Primary path: identity is XML', '// Fallback path: identity is plain whitespace-separated text — collocatePos is reused as a 1-based word index (same integer value, different counting scheme)'
The comment 'same integer value, different counting scheme' explicitly acknowledges the dual-semantic reuse
issues.suggestion: Extract the lambda body to a private method extractCollocateLemmaFromGroupIdentity(String identity, int collocatePos) with a Javadoc that explains the two-phase resolution (XML label path → plain-text fallback). The extracted method makes the dual-semantic reuse of collocatePos explicit in a named, testable unit rather than an anonymous capture.
issues.impact_scope: Developers modifying the surface-pattern extraction path must fully understand the XML vs plain-text identity ambiguity to avoid introducing bugs
issues.fix_scope: Extract 15-line lambda to a private named method in BlackLabQueryExecutor.java; update the single call site

issues.summary: Delegation/pass-through methods carry Javadocs longer than their bodies, with HTML-structured architecture rationale (h3, bold) on package-private helpers
evidence:
SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles(): 12-line Javadoc including '

Why this pass-through exists:' section for a 3-line method body that calls comparator.compareCollocateProfiles(seeds, opts) and wraps the IOException

MultiSeedExplorer.fetchCollocatesPerSeed() (package-private): Javadoc contains '

Query strategy: executeSurfacePattern only (intentionally no fallback)

' with sub-sections 'Design implication:' and 'Do not introduce a fallback here' — HTML-structured documentation on a method not visible outside the package

SingleSeedExplorer.fetchSeedCollocates() (private): mirrors the pattern with '

Query strategy: executeSurfacePattern primary, executeCollocations fallback

' and four '...: ' sub-sections
The cross-referencing between SingleSeedExplorer and MultiSeedExplorer Javadocs (each explaining why the other does not apply the same fallback) duplicates the same design rationale in two places
issues.suggestion: For delegation methods like compareCollocateProfiles: replace the multi-paragraph 'why this pass-through exists' Javadoc with a single sentence referencing the delegate: '@see CollocateProfileComparator#compareCollocateProfiles for the algorithm'. For the strategy Javadocs in fetchCollocatesPerSeed and fetchSeedCollocates: collapse to 2-3 plain sentences stating the key constraint (e.g., 'Uses executeSurfacePattern only — no fallback — to ensure cross-seed logDice scores are comparable. See MultiSeedExplorer.fetchCollocatesPerSeed for the symmetric rationale.'). Move the extended rationale to a package-level ADR comment or package-info.java if it must be preserved.
issues.impact_scope: Recurring across exploration package; developers reading these classes must scan through verbose HTML-formatted Javadocs to reach the actual algorithm
issues.fix_scope: Replace 3-4 verbose Javadoc blocks with short prose in the same files; no logic changes required

issues.summary: ExploreResponse interface retains discoveredNouns() after sourceSeeds rename, creating a permanent semantic mismatch for MultiSeed callers
evidence: ExploreResponse interface declares 'List<DiscoveredNounEntry> discoveredNouns()' with @JsonProperty('discovered_nouns')

ExploreResponse.MultiSeed record holds a 'sourceSeeds' field annotated @JsonProperty('source_seeds'); the discoveredNouns() override simply delegates: 'public List<DiscoveredNounEntry> discoveredNouns() { return sourceSeeds(); }'

The interface-level Javadoc acknowledges the semantic difference: 'In MultiSeed: the caller-supplied source seeds (input words), serialised under the source_seeds JSON key' — but the method name discoveredNouns() still implies discovery semantics that do not apply to MultiSeed
The sealed interface could support pattern-matching callers that access sourceSeeds() directly on MultiSeed without going through the bridge
issues.suggestion: Remove discoveredNouns() from the ExploreResponse sealed interface. Replace the two known call sites (ExploreResponseAssembler.buildDiscoveredNounEntries and ExploreResponseAssembler.buildExplorationEdges) with a sealed switch/instanceof that accesses SingleSeed.discoveredNouns() and MultiSeed.sourceSeeds() by their correct names. This removes the misleading bridge and makes the semantic distinction compile-time visible.
issues.impact_scope: Contained within the sealed ExploreResponse hierarchy; affects ExploreResponseAssembler call sites and any future code that holds a bare ExploreResponse reference
issues.fix_scope: Remove one interface method, add a sealed switch in ExploreResponseAssembler; update 2-3 call sites
